title: Synthesis owns iteration counter via .iteration-state.json sidecar
body: Convergent: integration + codex-adversary. Closes Codex major #5 (iteration off-by-one bug surface). Synthesis reads + increments .iteration-state.json; autorun bounds-checks (0 < iteration ≤ iteration_max + 1) at extract_and_decide; clean re-invocation removes the file. Persisted source-of-truth across manual re-runs / Synthesis retries / validator re-emits.

title: Persona-prompt drift detection via sentinel + checksum
body: Codex minor #8. <!-- BEGIN class-tagging -->...<!-- END class-tagging --> sentinels in every persona; tests/test-class-tagging-spliced.sh greps every persona for sentinels; CI fails on drift. Optional: generated checksum block for content drift detection.

title: Per-gate verdict sidecars; /build reads check-verdict.json hardcoded
body: Convergent: api + integration + codex-adversary. Files spec-review-verdict.json, plan-verdict.json, check-verdict.json. Fence label stays check-verdict for v1; stage field discriminates. Autorun's hardcoded path preserved. /build hardcoded to read check-verdict.json only — never falls through. Closes Codex blocker #2.

title: Test orchestrator wiring as named line item (recurring failure mode)
body: wave-sequencer. Per feedback_test_orchestrator_wiring_gap.md: parallel /build agents often forget to wire new tests. Wave 5 has explicit named task to edit tests/run-tests.sh. Verification: ls tests/test-*.sh | wc -l matches orchestrator's invocation list.

title: --dry-run-class-coverage flag mitigates persona-batch deadlock risk
body: Codex major #6. New gate flag reports which personas omit class: without producing real verdicts. Run before merging persona batch (Wave 2 → 3 transition). Prevents unclassified=block from deadlocking the rollout if any persona is missed.

title: Wave 1 carries the load-bearing autorun lockstep
body: wave-sequencer. Wave 1 = schemas + _policy_json.py + render-followups.py + _followups_lock.py + check.sh + CI guard. Indivisible. additionalProperties:false requires all 9 verdict fields atomically. Subsequent waves can be re-sequenced if scope cuts come; wave 1 is structurally indivisible.

title: 5-wave decomposition single-PR (data → personas template → behavior → docs → har
body: Wave-sequencer + integration. Single PR per autorun lockstep. 5 waves: contract+lockstep / persona-template-proof / behavior-closure / docs / hardening. USER APPROVAL GATE between wave 2 and wave 3 (template-first batching).

title: followups.jsonl class enum NARROWED to 4 (warn-routed only)
body: data-model. followups schema class: [contract, documentation, tests, scope-cuts]. architectural/security/unclassified never reach followups — they always block. Schema enforces narrowing; misclassified-into-followups bug fails validation.

title: cap_reached + NO_GO prints 3-line next-steps stderr block
body: ux. Opinionated default per feedback_obvious_decisions.md: 'Recommended: option 1 — architectural findings rarely improve on iteration.' Stays silent on cap_reached + GO_WITH_FIXES (user is unblocked).

title: Architectural carve-outs for v1 (data-loss/migration/rollback/supply-chain)
body: Codex major #7. Explicit carve-outs added: data-loss, irreversible-migration, release-rollback-failure, supply-chain-risk findings → class: architectural (block). v2 will give these own classes. Tiebreaker rule added to personas/judge.md.

title: Template-first persona batching with CI sentinel check
body: Convergent: integration + wave-sequencer + codex-adversary. New personas/_templates/class-tagging.md + scripts/apply-class-tagging-template.sh. Idempotency via <!-- BEGIN class-tagging --> sentinels. Wave 2 USER APPROVAL GATE before wave 3 27-file batch. tests/test-class-tagging-spliced.sh CI invariant test.

title: Persona-metrics back-fill: default class to unclassified at read
body: Convergent: data-model + integration + spec. 2-3 line addition to scripts/_render_persona_insights_text.py: row.get('class', 'unclassified') + filter for class != 'unclassified' in survival joins. NOT a migration sweep.

title: Strict v2-only schema bump (no oneOf branching)
body: Convergent: api + data-model + integration. _policy_json.py validator doesn't support oneOf (verified). Single-PR landing eliminates dual-version window. Refuse v1 verdicts after bump; CI guard catches partial landings.

title: Migration banner split: per-user + per-spec sentinels
body: Convergent: ux + stakeholders. ~/.claude/.gate-mode-default-flip-warned-v0.9.0 (per-user verbose ~5 lines once-ever) + docs/specs/<feature>/.gate-mode-warned (per-spec one-liner). Avoids banner blizzard for adopters with N stale specs.

title: Codex review at /check is BACKLOG-mandatory; add to spec body
body: security. /spec-review-level Codex doesn't satisfy /check-level Codex (different drift surface: plan-vs-codebase). Add line to spec: 'This spec's /check MUST include a Codex review pass per BACKLOG codex-review-per-spec.' Don't rely on inheritance.

title: Skip survival-classifier at /plan; defer to /wrap-insights
body: scalability. 37 findings × LLM call would be dominant cost. Spec marks non-blocking — correct. If wired into /wrap-insights Phase 1c, batch all into single LLM call (~5-10s).

title: Refuse --force-permissive in CI/AUTORUN env
body: Convergent: security + integration + scalability implicit. $CI or $AUTORUN_STAGE set → exit-with-error. Force-permissive is interactive escape-hatch only; mixing with automation = misuse. Hardens audit posture under headless invocation.

title: flock helper contract: fd-close release, no lock-file deletion
body: Codex major #3. Lock release via fd close (kernel handles death). Do NOT rely on lock-file deletion. Local-worktree assumption documented in helper docstring. NFS/iCloud explicitly out of scope.

title: fcntl.flock via Python helper for concurrency
body: Convergent: scalability + (security partial). New scripts/_followups_lock.py. Kernel auto-release on FD close (no PID-liveness needed). Lock-file content {pid, hostname, started_at} for audit only. 60s default timeout. NFS/iCloud out of scope.

title: class:security ↔ sev:security dual-emission via tags[] array
body: Convergent: api + data-model + security. Findings row gets class:security AND tags:[sev:security]. NOT severity-enum reuse (severity is orthogonal: blocker/major/minor/nit). Runtime parity in _policy_json.py _enforce_class_sev_parity(); mismatch coerces to unclassified.

title: install.sh upgrade banner via existing PRIOR_INSTALL block + sentinel
body: integration. Existing install.sh PRIOR_INSTALL detection (lines 283-305) is symmetric. Append one bullet to existing <<UPGRADE heredoc; gate on ~/.claude/.gate-permissiveness-migration-shown for one-shot semantics. PERSONA_METRICS_GITIGNORE block must add docs/specs/*/followups.jsonl.

title: followups.md rendered with provenance header + target_phase grouping
body: ux. Header: spec name, last gate, mode, iteration, links to JSONL + verdict.json. Sections by target_phase (build-inline, docs-only, plan-revision, post-build), then class. regression:true rows get ⚠ marker (only emoji exception). state:addressed/superseded hidden but counted.

title: commands/_gate-mode.md shared include for CLI flag truth table
body: api + integration. 24-cell truth table lives in one place; spec-review/plan/check commands include it. Identical mode resolution rules across the 3 gates. Truth table includes --strict --permissive ambiguity rejection.

title: Strict-mode stale followups + verdict block on NO_GO is sufficient
body: Codex major #4. Strict mode does NOT regenerate followups.jsonl (audit trail). state:open rows from prior permissive run remain visible. /build's verdict-check (must be GO/GO_WITH_FIXES) is the load-bearing guard — fires before followups consumption. Verdict's mode_source: cli-force makes override visible.

title: Tests deterministic-only — no claude -p in test path
body: scalability. tests/test-permissiveness.sh MUST NOT invoke claude -p. Pure deterministic logic; canned findings.jsonl fixtures fed to verdict-assembly only. Total budget <10s wall-clock. Pin as comment at top of script.

title: --force-permissive three-layer disclosure (banner + JSONL audit + /wrap)
body: ux + security. Stderr banner at invocation; JSONL audit row to .force-permissive-log; /wrap-quick surface defer-able to v0.9.1. Reason string mandatory: --force-permissive="releasing hotfix". Captured in audit log.

title: /build legacy-detection ladder for v0.9.0 transition
body: Codex blocker #1. Explicit branch: missing sidecar (pre-v0.9.0) → today's behavior; v1 sidecar → refuse with upgrade pointer; v2 GO/GO_WITH_FIXES → proceed; v2 NO_GO → refuse. All 4 paths fixture-tested. Prevents collapse into either 'always proceed' or 'always fail.'

title: class:unclassified fail-closed fallback HARDCODED as block
body: Convergent: security + data-model + spec already includes. unclassified == block is HARDCODED, not configurable via constitution. Constitution attempting to demote → constitution-validation failure. Closes the silent security demotion path.
